one_line_takeaway: DynHD detects diffusion-LLM hallucinations by modeling how filtered token-entropy evolves across denoising steps and flagging deviations from a query-conditioned 'factual' reference trajectory, beating the prior SOTA (TraceDet) by 12.2 AUROC points on LLaDA-8B.
key_finding: DynHD is the strongest detector in almost all settings. On LLaDA-8B-Instruct it beats the previous SOTA TraceDet by an average of 12.2 absolute AUROC points (DynHD avg 84.2% vs TraceDet 72.0%); on Dream-7B-Instruct it reaches 84.3% avg (vs TraceDet 80.8%). It generalizes better zero-shot across datasets (avg cross-task AUROC 72.9% vs TraceDet 66.3%, and 85.5% vs 73.1% on the TriviaQA->HotpotQA transfer). Ablations show token filtering is the single most important component (removing it drops LLaDA TriviaQA-128 from 86.7% to 74.8%), and that naive temporal averaging collapses performance (down to ~57%), confirming that the learned temporal attention and reference-deviation modeling carry most of the gain. The paper's core empirical claim is that the SHAPE/dynamics of the entropy trajectory (smooth monotonic decay for factual samples vs late-stage stagnation or rebound for hallucinations), not the absolute uncertainty at the final step, is the discriminative signal.
core_contribution: The paper proposes DynHD, a supervised post-hoc hallucination detector purpose-built for diffusion large language models (D-LLMs, e.g. LLaDA-8B, Dream-7B). Its OWN contribution has two parts. (1) A semantic-aware evidence construction module: a rule-based filter removes non-informative tokens (control/boundary markers such as <|endoftext|>/[PAD], punctuation/whitespace, task boilerplate like 'Answer:', stopwords, and subword fragments) so only semantically meaningful positions K_t remain, then per denoising step the surviving token entropies are condensed into a 3-D statistical evidence vector a_t = (mean, max, top-k) of entropy. (2) A dynamical deviation learning module: a query-conditioned 'reference evidence dynamics generator' g_theta, trained ONLY on factual samples (y=0), predicts the expected normal entropy-evidence trajectory; a deviation-based detector then builds x_t=[a_t; a_hat_t; delta a_t], applies a shared MLP + learnable temporal-attention weights omega_t, and classifies from the weighted-pooled representation, with two regularizers that penalize late-stage stagnation (L_path, path-deviation) and uncertainty rebound (L_reb). The framing distinguishes itself from prior D-LLM detector TraceDet (Chang et al., 2026) on two axes: spatial (token information-density imbalance, ignored by TraceDet) and temporal (continuous denoising dynamics, which TraceDet only samples at a few key steps).
threat_model: N/A. This is not an adversarial-security paper and defines no attacker. 'Hallucination' here means unintentional factual errors produced by the model's own generation process, not an induced or adversarial failure. The task is framed strictly as binary classification of question-response pairs into factual (y=0) vs hallucinated (y=1). No adversary, attack surface, or defense-against-manipulation is modeled.
limitations: (1) Not a mitigation - it is a post-hoc detector requiring a supervised, labeled training set (1,400 labeled samples per setting), unlike zero-resource baselines. (2) Ground-truth labels are produced by an LLM judge (GPT-4o-mini), so measured performance is against LLM-annotated factuality, not gold human labels (94% agreement is reported but the judge is imperfect). (3) It underperforms on constrained multiple-choice / commonsense settings: on the CSQA-sourced cross-task transfers DynHD can trail TraceDet (e.g. 73.6% vs 74.8% on CSQA->TriviaQA), which the authors attribute to fixed answer options producing faster decisions and fewer observable uncertainty signals; output-based/latent baselines also note that multiple-choice answers are 'confident but wrong.' (4) Scope is narrow: only two D-LLM backbones, three fact-QA datasets, and a single metric (AUROC); no evaluation on long-form generation, code, or non-QA tasks. (5) The method presupposes access to the full per-step entropy trajectory of the D-LLM, i.e. white-box internal access to the denoising process.

quantitative_results: LLaDA-8B-Instruct: DynHD avg AUROC 84.2% vs TraceDet 72.0% (+12.2 absolute)
Dream-7B-Instruct: DynHD avg AUROC 84.3% vs TraceDet 80.8%
Zero-shot cross-task avg AUROC: DynHD 72.9% vs TraceDet 66.3% (TSV 61.3%, CCS 58.2%)
Ablation - w/o token filtering: LLaDA TriviaQA-128 drops 86.7% -> 74.8%
Ablation - Naive Average temporal aggregation collapses to ~57.3% (LLaDA TriviaQA-128)
